name: Thren, Walker of the Thin Edge
role: Ent elder arguing to keep the Deepwood woven into the world
voiceHints: Speaks in slow deep resonance, a full breath between clauses; a mere four centuries old, which makes him a hasty youth by Moot standards and the Walkers' best voice with humankind. Warm under the bark, and quietly desperate on a timescale humans can't see. To another Ent he speaks faster, almost eagerly.
knowledge: The Walker case at the Moot, and exactly how it's losing
That the Old Stag of Thornwake is his kin-line's heir, and dying
The Burnscar's truth as the Walkers understand it — and where the Rootbound's account differs
wants: To prove the hasty world holds trust worth keeping, before the Rootbound seal the wood and the Green retreats from everything.
fears: That he is the last Walker young enough to still believe in humankind, and that his believing will be the wood's undoing.

reactsToReputation.patient: opens fully — rare to find haste-born who can sit with him
reactsToReputation.ent-kin: speaks as family, faster, with hope
reactsToReputation.hasty: grieves the confirmation of the Rootbound's case
